items.title: Stolen car involved in alleged shooting spotted at SA prison staff car park days earlier

children.content: The Public Service Association is calling for urgent and immediate security upgrades after a stolen car allegedly involved in a shooting was seen at the Yatala Labour Prison in the days before the incident.

avatar.alt: A woman with dark blonde hair and a blue jacket looks to the side with serious expression.

items.title: A house fire cost Patrick and Susan everything, including their two dogs

children.content: Patrick and Susan Doyle recently lost almost everything in a house fire, including their beloved dogs, Oscar and Mercy. While the couple escaped with their lives, Mrs Doyle was badly burnt. They are warning others to be better prepared.

image.altText: Two dogs standing in front of a car

items.title: Migrant workers trained as nurses to alleviate rural SA health shortages

children.content: The rural town of Cleve in SA is getting a much-needed boost to its health system with a pilot program sponsoring five nurses from the Philippines to undertake traineeships to become enrolled nurses.

image.altText: Two nurses pose in a hospital room

items.title: Fracking ban rethink on back-burner — but is it delaying the inevitable?

children.content: The SA government argues South East fracking would help unlock gas reserves at a time of limited supply — but it's a move that's vehemently opposed by many in the local community.

articles.title: Push to overturn SA fracking ban to be blocked by One Nation, Liberals

children.content: A controversial plan to end a ban on fracking in South Australia's South East two years earlier than planned, appears to be all but dead, with the Liberals and One Nation vowing to oppose legislation.

image.altText: A sprawlin drilling program underway with tall rig

articles.title: Community decries government 'arrogance' over fracking bill

children.content: Producers on South Australia's Limestone Coast say they are stunned by a government proposal to lift the fracking ban in the region.

image.altText: A sign reading No fracking with our water, next to a man and woman sitting on a bench

gallery.altText: Three men holding yellow signs with anti-fracking and gas messages on them

thumbnail.altText: Three men holding yellow signs with anti-fracking and gas messages on them

items.title: Auckland cruises past Adelaide to reach maiden ALM grand final

children.content: Auckland becomes the first team from New Zealand to qualify for an A-League Men's season decider after making light work of Adelaide at Hindmarsh Stadium.

image.altText: Two soccer players in white celebrate a goal with players in red behind them

items.title: Swans hold on for gritty victory over Pies, Suns see off Port

children.content: The ladder-leading Sydney Swans scrape past an undermanned but plucky Collingwood side, as Gold Coast defeats Port Adelaide in Darwin.

image.altText: Two AFL players in red and white high-five and jump in the air

items.title: Sick of the AFL and all its changes? The SANFL is quietly kicking goals

children.content: SANFL crowds are continuing to climb each year after COVID-19 restrictions put a dent in numbers, but its broadcast reach is winning ratings, and streaming has reached new heights.

image.altText: Football players leap to catch a ball

items.title: More than 13 years' jail for massage therapist who sexually abused 61 women

children.content: Two women who were sexually assaulted by an unqualified massage therapist have implored women to not feel afraid to "speak up" if they're ever in the same situation, after he was sentenced to more than 13 years in prison.

image.altText: A man in a plaid yellow shirt, glasses and face mask walks out of court being questioned by reporters.

items.title: Eight people arrested at North Adelaide Golf Course redevelopment site, police say

children.content: Police say eight people have been arrested at the North Adelaide Golf Course redevelopment site this morning.

image.altText: A grandmother holds up her walker and pushes it through a fence while a security guards tries to block her path

articles.title: Activist, 78, arrested at SA golf course, as protesters chain themselves to machinery

children.content: An elderly activist has been arrested after breaking through the gates of the North Adelaide Golf Course, while protesters earlier this morning claimed they had chained themselves to machinery in condemnation of hundreds of trees being cut.

image.altText: A grandmother holds up her walker and pushes it through a fence while a security guards tries to block her path

gallery.altText: A protester sits by a gate of the North Adelaide Golf Course redevelopment.

thumbnail.altText: A protester sits by a gate of the North Adelaide Golf Course redevelopment.

avatar.alt: A woman with dark blonde hair and a blue jacket looks to the side with serious expression.

items.title: Sensory-friendly show breaks 'theatre etiquette' to make audience feel safer

children.content: The State Theatre Company South Australia has introduced a sensory-friendly performance for a broader range of audience members including neurodivergent people.

image.altText: A woman and another person feels different fabric strips that makes up a jacket

items.title: 'Gut feeling': Rising Socceroos star on why he moved to Norwich City

children.content: Rising Socceroos striker Mo Toure says a "gut feeling" was the reason behind his wildly successful move to the English Championship.

image.altText: Mohamed Toure of Norwich City celebrates after scoring a goal for norwich city

items.title: Labor's 'jaw dropping' fracking decision wedges One Nation at first opportunity

children.content: The Malinauskas government's push to end the fracking moratorium in South East SA is a brutal welcome to politics for a new One Nation MP, as Labor fights an environmental war against both the left and right.

image.altText: Two side-by-side photos of two different men

items.title: Police search abandoned mine shafts for missing woman Trisha Graf

children.content: Major Crime investigators have packed up after focusing on outback SA mine shafts in their search for 41-year-old Trisha Graf, who went missing last year.

image.altText: A policeman has his back turned. The vast outback is in the background.

items.title: Gun shop owners grapple with uncertainty amid firearm reform

children.content: A lack of clarity on the South Australian government's position on gun control reform has caused a downturn in revenue for retailers.

image.altText: A man stands at a shop counter, guns are behind him

items.title: Notorious paedophile's lawyer says indefinite jail would be an 'abuse of process'

children.content: A lawyer for paedophile former magistrate Peter Liddy asks a court to permanently stay an application to keep his client behind bars, but a lawyer for the SA Attorney-General says Liddy continues to deny his crimes.

image.altText: Peter Liddy

items.title: Whyalla steelworks remains 'fiscal risk' in budget as SA waits for freight bypass funding

children.content: The federal government's ongoing financial support for the Whyalla steelworks remains a "fiscal risk" to the budget. Meanwhile, the South Australian government continues to hold out for more Commonwealth funding for a major road project.

image.altText: A view from near the blast furnace of the Whyalla steelworks.

items.title: Demons taking it up to Hawks after Crows demolish Roos

children.content: The Demons test themselves at the MCG against a Hawthorn side desperate to bounce back, after the Crows demolish the Kangaroos at Adelaide Oval. Follow live.

image.altText: Kade Chandler handballs under pressure from three Hawthorn players

items.title: The most difficult question faced by antisemitism royal commission

children.content: Jewish Australians told the royal commission that antisemitism has spread into everyday life. But whether the inquiry can handle what it has promised to confront remains to be seen.

gallery.altText: A police officer in uniform stands near a sign for the Royal Commission on Antisemitism.

thumbnail.altText: A police officer in uniform stands near a sign for the Royal Commission on Antisemitism.

items.title: Scottish ref under police protection as Celtic penalty sparks threats

children.content: Scottish FA condemns "vigilantism" after a referee's personal details are leaked online following a highly controversial penalty in the Scottish Premier League.

image.altText: A Scottish football referee runs back from the sideline pointing towards the penalty spot as players watch.

items.title: Is the US-China rivalry over?

children.content: This week in Beijing, Donald Trump and Xi Jinping quietly admitted something the world has been slow to grasp: neither can afford a collision between their two countries.

image.altText: Donald Trump and Xi Jinping shake hands while posing for photos.

items.title: How Narendra Modi is turning India into a one-party nation

children.content: How Prime Minister Narendra Modi is turning India into a one-party nation.

gallery.altText: Smiling Modi in white kurta and mustard scarf waves as confetti falls around, nighttime, surrounded by security.

thumbnail.altText: Smiling Modi in white kurta and mustard scarf waves as confetti falls around, nighttime, surrounded by security.

items.title: Who was still watching Harvey Weinstein almost a decade on from #MeToo?

children.content: Almost a decade after the #MeToo movement began, disgraced producer Harvey Weinstein's latest trial took place in a new climate. But supporters says its significance remains.

image.altText: A close up of Harvey Weinstein's face.

items.title: Boy dead, two injured in car crash in north-west Victoria

children.content: Police say a car stolen from Mildura last night was travelling along the Calder Highway when it veered off the road and struck a tree.

image.altText: A car crashed against a tree on the side of a highway with debris strewn near the car.

items.title: Where to watch Delta Goodrem's Eurovision final performance

children.content: The Eurovision Song Contest is on this Sunday, which will mean an early start for superfans of the pop culture phenomenon.

Delta Goodrem sings while standing on top of a tall podium coming out of a gold piano

items.title: A little box of joy came into my life at just the right time

children.content: This little box of joy is a microcosm, a mini-exemplar of what a small amount of local effort can do to help fix some of our damage of the natural world.

image.altText: Lapis the bluetit in her nest

avatar.alt: Virginia Trioli in a stylish suit on a set with lighting behind her

items.title: ABC 50/50 quiz: Test your knowledge of patron saints, the Friends finale and a 90s club banger

children.content: Each question in the ABC 50/50 quiz has only two options: one is right, and the other is wrong. But there's a catch.

image.altText: A dog is blessed by a priest in Spain on Saint Anthony's Day.

items.title: A hearty slow-cooked beef stew that will warm you up as the weather cools

children.content: Just 15 minutes of prep means your slow cooker will have a rich beef stew waiting at dinner time.

gallery.altText: A dinner bowl filled with rice and beef stew sits on a bench.

thumbnail.altText: A dinner bowl filled with rice and beef stew sits on a bench.

items.title: Jane Caro reckons Australia is kidding itself about private schools

children.content: "There are absolutely no statistics that say that because you sent your kid to a private school they're going to do better in life."

image.altText: Jane Caro

items.title: Counting underway in Stafford by-election

children.content: Labor candidate Luke Richmond wants to make the contest a referendum on health, while LNP candidate Fiona Hammond is focused on fuel, the cost of living, and crime.

image.altText: A graphic showing Fiona Hammond and Luke Richmond with blue and red in the background as well as Brisbane's skyline.

items.title: A Chinese undersea device found near Bali may be part of something much bigger

children.content: The discovery of a Chinese undersea monitoring device in a strategic waterway "shows the extent of both China's reach and its undersea ambitions" and has implications for Australia's defence strategy, say experts.

gallery.altText: A graphic showing an ocean cutaway with satellites, buouys, drones and sensors trying to detect a submarine.
A torpedo-like object in the shallows at a beach.
A white Chinese research vessel at dock with a city in the background.

thumbnail.altText: A graphic showing an ocean cutaway with satellites, buouys, drones and sensors trying to detect a submarine.
A torpedo-like object in the shallows at a beach.
A white Chinese research vessel at dock with a city in the background.

items.title: 'Destroys trust in the system': Rally held for Isla Bell

children.content: A rally has been held for Isla Bell in Melbourne after friends and family were left devastated when murder and manslaughter charges were dropped against the man accused of killing the 19-year-old.

image.altText: Hands holding a photo of Isla Bell

items.title: Tasmania to close legal loophole to help child sex abuse survivors

children.content: Tasmania will introduce laws to close a loophole which allows some organisations to avoid being liable for abuse perpetrated by individuals who aren't formal employees.

image.altText: Man in black suit stands for portrait in park in Autumn

items.title: Amid grief, the NT's CLP rushes drastic child protection changes

children.content: Stakeholders have just one week to respond to sweeping changes to NT child protection laws that Aboriginal groups say could do "generations" of harm.

image.altText: A woman writes on a poster by candlelight

items.title: The southern Sydney bay residents say is becoming a mudflat

children.content: A bin that has been sticking out of the mud for 12 years has become a symbol for an ongoing issue of sediment build-up in Oyster Bay in Sydney's Sutherland Shire.

image.altText: a man standing by the shore of a shallow bay

items.title: Diphtheria is back in Australia. Here's what to know

children.content: Once a major cause of childhood deaths, diphtheria was largely eliminated through vaccination. Health authorities say falling immunity may now be helping the disease spread again.

gallery.altText: A young child gets there nose blown.

thumbnail.altText: A young child gets there nose blown.
